page: Amazon Web Services r8g.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the ARM64 architecture, this instance features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The underlying hardware utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor, and the instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark results highlight the instance's exceptional memory subsystem performance. The r8g.8xlarge achieves top-tier status in memory bandwidth tests, recording 402.49 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 183.47 GB/sec for large-block write operations. This high-throughput memory architecture is reflected in its Passmark memory score and database operations, both of which rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core processing is also highly competitive, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21614.0, which also places in the top tier.

The instance demonstrates strong performance in in-memory data store operations and web serving, processing 66.65 million Redis SET operations per second and delivering 74.43 GB/sec of static web serving throughput. However, workloads requiring heavy vector or matrix extensions may experience limitations due to the absence of AVX and AMX instruction sets on the ARM64 architecture. Additionally, large-scale LLM inference is a notable tradeoff, with prompt processing for a 70B model registering a weak 5.9 tokens/sec. The r8g.8xlarge offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads, making it highly suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r8g.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical ARM64 cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB of memory per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark evaluations, the r8g.8xlarge delivers top-tier memory performance, achieving 402.49 GB/sec in large-block read bandwidth and ranking in the top 10% for database operations and multi-core Geekbench scores. It also demonstrates strong throughput in Redis operations and static web serving. However, it exhibits weaker performance in large LLM inference tasks. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring high memory bandwidth.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.8xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 32 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth for databases and caching.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.8xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance with 32 Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized ARM64 instance delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and high-density resource allocation for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors.
256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core.
Top-tier memory bandwidth reaching 402.49 GB/sec for large-block reads.
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration.
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
